name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
وَكَأَيِّن مِّن قَرْيَةٍ عَتَتْ عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهَا وَرُسُلِهِۦ فَحَاسَبْنَـٰهَا حِسَابًا شَدِيدًا وَعَذَّبْنَـٰهَا عَذَابًا نُّكْرًا (٨)
```

```english
How many a town defied the command of its Lord and His apostles, then We called it to a severe account and punished it with a dire punishment. (8)
```

```arabic
فَذَاقَتْ وَبَالَ أَمْرِهَا وَكَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ أَمْرِهَا خُسْرًا (٩)
```

```english
So it tasted the evil consequences of its conduct, and the outcome of its conduct was ruin. (9)
```

```arabic
أَعَدَّ ٱللَّهُ لَهُمْ عَذَابًا شَدِيدًا ۖ فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ يَـٰٓأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ۚ قَدْ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ إِلَيْكُمْ ذِكْرًا (١٠)
```

```english
Allah has prepared for them a severe punishment. So be wary of Allah, O you who possess intellect and have faith! Allah has already sent down to you a reminder, (10)
```

```arabic
رَّسُولًا يَتْلُوا۟ عَلَيْكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ مُبَيِّنَـٰتٍ لِّيُخْرِجَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ مِنَ ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتِ إِلَى ٱلنُّورِ ۚ وَمَن يُؤْمِنۢ بِٱللَّهِ وَيَعْمَلْ صَـٰلِحًا يُدْخِلْهُ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَآ أَبَدًا ۖ قَدْ أَحْسَنَ ٱللَّهُ لَهُۥ رِزْقًا (١١)
```

```english
an apostle reciting to you the manifest signs of Allah that He may bring out those who have faith and do righteous deeds from darkness into light. And whoever has faith in Allah and does righteous deeds, He shall admit him into gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them forever. Allah has certainly granted him an excellent provision. (11)
```

```arabic
ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ سَبْعَ سَمَـٰوَٰتٍ وَمِنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ مِثْلَهُنَّ يَتَنَزَّلُ ٱلْأَمْرُ بَيْنَهُنَّ لِتَعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ قَدْ أَحَاطَ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عِلْمًۢا (١٢)
```

```english
It is Allah who has created seven heavens, and of the earth [a number] similar to them. The command gradually descends through them, that you may know that Allah has power over all things, and that Allah comprehends all things in knowledge. (12)
```

## (Commentary)

This is an admonition, a warning, and a glad tiding that reinforces the recommendation to adhere to the laws Allah has prescribed for them. Among these are the laws He prescribed regarding divorce and the waiting period (العدة) — *the prescribed period a woman must observe after divorce or the death of her husband before she can remarry*. The Noble Quran has not recommended nor emphasized any of the prescribed laws as much as it has recommended and emphasized the laws concerning women, and this is only because their matter is of great significance.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَكَأَيِّنْ مِنْ قَرْيَةٍ عَتَتْ عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّها وَرُسُلِهِ فَحاسَبْناها حِساباً شَدِيداً وَعَذَّبْناها عَذاباً نُكْراً** "How many a town defied the command of its Lord and His apostles! So We called it to a severe reckoning and punished it with a dire punishment." (65:8). Al-Raghib said: "Defiance (العتو) is to recoil from obedience." Thus, it is close in meaning to arrogance (الاستكبار). He also said: "Dire (النكر) refers to cunning and a difficult matter that is unrecognized." What is meant by "dire" (النكر) in the verse is the second meaning. In *al-Majma‘*, it is said: "Dire (النكر) is that which is abominable and horrific, the likes of which has not been seen."

The "town" (القرية) refers to its inhabitants by way of metaphor (المجاز), as in His saying: **وَسْئَلِ الْقَرْيَةَ** "Ask the town" (12:82). In His saying: **عَتَتْ عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّها وَرُسُلِهِ** "defied the command of its Lord and His apostles," there is an indication that they disbelieved in Allah, the Glorified, through polytheism (الشرك), and they committed another act of disbelief against His messengers by denying them in their call. Furthermore, they disbelieved in Allah, the Exalted, by abandoning His prescribed laws, and they disbelieved in His messengers regarding what they were commanded of their guardianship (الولاية), as previously mentioned in the like of His saying: **وَأَطِيعُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ فَإِنْ تَوَلَّيْتُمْ فَإِنَّما عَلى رَسُولِنَا الْبَلاغُ الْمُبِينُ** "Obey Allah and obey the Apostle; but if you turn away, then Our Apostle’s duty is only to communicate in clear terms." (64:12).

The "severity of reckoning" (شدة الحساب) refers to being meticulous in it and exhaustive in order to fulfill the recompense exactly as it is. What is meant here is the reckoning of this world, not the reckoning of the Hereafter. The evidence for it being the reckoning of this world is His saying, the Exalted: **وَما أَصابَكُمْ مِنْ مُصِيبَةٍ فَبِما كَسَبَتْ أَيْدِيكُمْ وَيَعْفُوا عَنْ كَثِيرٍ** "Whatever affliction that may visit you is because of what your hands have earned, and He excuses many [an offense]." (42:30), and His saying: **وَلَوْ أَنَّ أَهْلَ الْقُرى آمَنُوا وَاتَّقَوْا لَفَتَحْنا عَلَيْهِمْ بَرَكاتٍ مِنَ السَّماءِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَلكِنْ كَذَّبُوا فَأَخَذْناهُمْ بِما كانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ** "If the people of the towns had been faithful and God-wary, We would have opened to them blessings from the heaven and the earth. But they denied; so We seized them because of what they used to earn." (7:96).

Thus, whatever affliction befalls a human being—and it is an affliction from the perspective of religion—is the result of the reckoning of his deeds. Allah pardons many of them through indulgence and leniency in the reckoning, except that He, the Exalted, reckons the defiant ones who are arrogant toward His command and His messengers with a severe reckoning through meticulousness, exhaustion, and rebuke, and then punishes them with a dire punishment.

The meaning is: How many inhabitants of a town defied and were arrogant toward the command of their Lord and His messengers, failing to obey Allah and His messengers, so We called them to a severe reckoning in which We were meticulous and exhaustive, and We punished them with a difficult, unprecedented punishment, which is the punishment of extirpation (عذاب الاستئصال) in this world.

As for the claim that what is meant is the punishment of the Hereafter, and that the use of the past tense is to indicate the certainty of its occurrence, it is not sound.

In His saying: **فَحاسَبْناها حِساباً شَدِيداً وَعَذَّبْناها** "So We called it to a severe reckoning and punished it," there is a transition (الالتفات) from the third person to the first person plural, the point of which is to indicate grandeur (العظمة).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **فَذاقَتْ وَبالَ أَمْرِها وَكانَ عاقِبَةُ أَمْرِها خُسْراً** "So it tasted the evil consequence of its conduct, and the outcome of its conduct was loss." (65:9). What is meant by "its conduct" (أمرها) is its defiance and arrogance. The meaning is: The punishment for their defiance befell them, and the end of their defiance was loss (الخسر), as if they had purchased defiance at the cost of obedience, which resulted in their loss.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **أَعَدَّ اللهُ لَهُمْ عَذاباً شَدِيداً** "Allah has prepared for them a severe punishment." (65:10). This is their recompense in the Hereafter, just as what was in His saying: "So We called it to a severe reckoning and punished it with a dire punishment... so it tasted the evil consequence of its conduct" was their recompense in this world.

The benefit in His saying: "Allah has prepared for them..." etc., is that it stands in the position of answering a potential question (دفع الدخل). It is as if when it was said: "and the outcome of its conduct was loss," it was asked: "What is meant by their loss?" So it was said: "Allah has prepared for them a severe punishment."

**His saying, the Exalted:** **فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ يا أُولِي الْأَلْبابِ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا قَدْ أَنْزَلَ اللهُ إِلَيْكُمْ ذِكْراً** "So be wary of Allah, O you who possess intellect and have faith! Allah has already sent down to you a reminder," (65:10). This is a conclusion drawn from what preceded, addressed to the believers so that they may take precaution and protect themselves from defying the command of their Lord and transgressing against His obedience, lest they be afflicted with the evil consequence of their defiance and the loss of their outcome, just as the destroyed towns were afflicted.

He described the believers as "those who possess intellect" (أولي الألباب) when He said: "So be wary of Allah, O you who possess intellect and have faith," seeking support from their intellects (العقول) for the godwariness (التقوى) — *the state of being conscious of Allah's presence that protects one from sin* — He desires from them. For when they heard that a people defied the command of their Lord and were thus called to a severe reckoning and punished with a dire punishment, and the outcome of their conduct was loss, and then they heard that this was repeated time after time, destroying people after people, their intellects dictated that defiance and arrogance toward the command of Allah is an exposure to the severe reckoning of Allah and His dire punishment. Thus, it alerts them and rouses them toward godwariness. Allah has indeed sent down to them a reminder (الذكر) to remind them of what is for them and what is against them, and to guide them to the truth and to a straight path (الصراط المستقيم).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **رَسُولاً يَتْلُوا عَلَيْكُمْ آياتِ اللهِ مُبَيِّناتٍ** "an apostle who recites to you the clarifying signs of Allah," (65:11) etc. This is an explanatory apposition (عطف بيان) or a substitute (البدل) for "a reminder" (ذكراً). Thus, what is meant by the "reminder" that He sent down is the Messenger; he is called by this name because he is the means of reminding (التذكرة) of Allah and His signs, and the way of calling to the religion of truth. What is meant by the Messenger is Muhammad (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), as supported by the apparent meaning of His saying: "who recites to you the clarifying signs of Allah," etc.

Based on this, what is meant by "sending down" (إنزال) the Messenger is his being commissioned from the realm of the Unseen (عالم الغيب) and his manifestation to them as a messenger from Him after they had not expected it, as in His saying: **وَأَنْزَلْنَا الْحَدِيدَ** "And We sent down iron" (57:25).

The apparent sense of "sending down" (الإنزال) being from the heaven led some, such as the author of *al-Kashshaf*, to interpret "Messenger" (رسولاً) as Gabriel. In that case, the meaning of his reciting the signs to them would be his reciting them to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) insofar as he is the one followed by his people and the means of communicating to them. However, the apparent meaning of His saying: "recites to you" (يتلو عليكم) etc., is contrary to that.

It is also possible that "Messenger" (رسولاً) is in the accusative case due to a hidden verb, the estimation being: "He sent a messenger who recites to you the signs of Allah." In this case, the "reminder" sent down to them would be the Quran or the laws and gnosis (المعارف) explained therein.

His saying: **لِيُخْرِجَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ مِنَ الظُّلُماتِ إِلَى النُّورِ** "so that He may bring out those who have faith and do righteous deeds from darkness into light" (65:11) has had its explanation preceded in similar instances.

His saying: **وَمَنْ يُؤْمِنْ بِاللهِ وَيَعْمَلْ صالِحاً يُدْخِلْهُ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها أَبَداً** "And whoever has faith in Allah and acts righteously, He will admit him into gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them forever" (65:11) is a beautiful promise and glad tidings.

His saying: **قَدْ أَحْسَنَ اللهُ لَهُ رِزْقاً** "Allah has certainly granted him an excellent provision" (65:11) is a description of His excellence, the Exalted, toward them in the provision He has granted them. What is meant by "provision" (الرزق) is what He provided them of faith and righteous deeds in this world, and Paradise in the Hereafter. It is also said that what is meant by it is Paradise.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **اللهُ الَّذِي خَلَقَ سَبْعَ سَماواتٍ وَمِنَ الْأَرْضِ مِثْلَهُنَّ يَتَنَزَّلُ الْأَمْرُ بَيْنَهُنَ** "It is Allah who has created seven heavens, and of the earth [a number] similar to them. The command gradually descends through them," (65:12) etc. This is an exposition that reinforces what preceded in the verses regarding the matter of His Lordship (الربوبية), the commissioning of the Messenger, and His sending down the Reminder so that they may obey Him therein; and that in rebellion and opposition to Him is the severe reckoning and painful punishment, while in His obedience is the eternal Paradise. All of this is because He is All-Powerful (قدير), All-Knowing (عليم).

So His saying: **اللهُ الَّذِي خَلَقَ سَبْعَ سَماواتٍ** "It is Allah who has created seven heavens" has had some discussion regarding it in the commentary of Surah Ha Mim al-Sajdah.

His saying: **وَمِنَ الْأَرْضِ مِثْلَهُنَ** "and of the earth [a number] similar to them," its apparent meaning is similarity in number. Accordingly, the meaning is: He created seven of the earth just as He created seven of the heaven. Are the seven earths seven spheres of the same type as the earth we are on, of which ours is one? Or is the earth we are on composed of seven layers encompassing one another, the highest layer being its surface upon which we reside? Or does it refer to the seven climes (الأقاليم السبعة) into which they divided the inhabited part of the sphere's surface? These are views held by various groups, and perhaps by referring back to what preceded in the commentary of Surah Ha Mim al-Sajdah, another possibility besides these may appear.

It has also been said that what is meant by His saying: "and of the earth [a number] similar to them" is that He created from the earth something that is like the seven heavens, which is the human being, composed of earthly matter and a heavenly spirit (الروح) that contains heavenly, celestial (ملكوتية) archetypes.

His saying: **يَتَنَزَّلُ الْأَمْرُ بَيْنَهُنَ** "The command gradually descends through them," the apparent meaning is that the pronoun refers to both the heavens and the earth. The "command" (الأمر) is the divine command which He explained by His saying: **إِنَّما أَمْرُهُ إِذا أَرادَ شَيْئاً أَنْ يَقُولَ لَهُ كُنْ** "All His command, when He wills something, is to say to it ‘Be,’ and it is." (36:83). It is the word of origination (كلمة الإيجاد). Its "descending" (تنزله) is its taking the path of descent from the source of the command to one heaven after another until it reaches the earthly world, whereby what was intended by the command—be it an entity, an effect, provision, death, life, honor, humiliation, or otherwise—comes into being. The Exalted said: **وَأَوْحى فِي كُلِّ سَماءٍ أَمْرَها** "and He revealed to each heaven its command" (41:12), and He said: **يُدَبِّرُ الْأَمْرَ مِنَ السَّماءِ إِلَى الْأَرْضِ ثُمَّ يَعْرُجُ إِلَيْهِ فِي يَوْمٍ كانَ مِقْدارُهُ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ مِمَّا تَعُدُّونَ** "He directs the command from the heaven to the earth, then it ascends to Him in a day whose span is a thousand years by your reckoning." (32:5).

It is said that what is meant by the "command" is the legislative command (الأمر التشريعي); the angels of revelation descend with it from the heaven to the Prophet while he is on the earth. This is a specification (التخصيص) without a specifier, and the conclusion of the verse: "so that you may know that Allah..." etc., does not suit it.

His saying: **أَنَّ اللهَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ وَأَنَّ اللهَ قَدْ أَحاطَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عِلْماً** "so that you may know that Allah has power over all things and that Allah comprehends all things in knowledge." (65:12). This is among the objectives resulting from the creation of the seven heavens and of the earth a similar number, and His sending down the command through them. In this is the attribution of creation (الخلق) and command (الأمر) to Him and their exclusivity to Him. For one who reflects upon that will not doubt His power over all things and His knowledge of all things. Therefore, let the believers who possess intellect be wary of opposing His command, for the sunnah (السنة) — *the established way or law* — of this All-Powerful, All-Knowing One proceeds toward rewarding those who obey His commands and punishing the defiant and arrogant. "Such is the seizing of your Lord when He seizes the towns while they are wrongdoing; indeed, His seizing is painful and severe."

## (Narrative Discussion)

In the *Tafsir* of al-Qummi: Regarding His saying, the Exalted: **وَكَأَيِّنْ مِنْ قَرْيَةٍ** "How many a town," he said: "The people of the town."

In *Tafsir al-Burhan*, from Ibn Babawayh with his chain of narration from al-Rayyan ibn al-Salt from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him) in the discourse of al-Ma'mun, he said: "The Reminder (الذكر) is the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) and we are his family (أهله). That is clear in the Book of Allah where He says in Surah al-Talaq: **فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ يا أُولِي الْأَلْبابِ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ـ قَدْ أَنْزَلَ اللهُ إِلَيْكُمْ ذِكْراً ـ رَسُولاً يَتْلُوا عَلَيْكُمْ آياتِ اللهِ مُبَيِّناتٍ** 'So be wary of Allah, O you who possess intellect and have faith! Allah has already sent down to you a reminder, an apostle who recites to you the clarifying signs of Allah.' So the Reminder is the Messenger of Allah and we are his family."

In the *Tafsir* of al-Qummi: My father narrated to me from al-Husayn ibn Khalid from Abu al-Hasan al-Rida (peace be upon him), who said: I said to him: "Inform me about the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: **وَالسَّماءِ ذاتِ الْحُبُكِ** 'By the heaven with its tracks' (51:7)." He said: "**هي محبوكة إلى الأرض** (It is woven to the earth)," and he interlaced his fingers. I said: "How can it be woven to the earth when Allah says: **رَفَعَ السَّماواتِ بِغَيْرِ عَمَدٍ تَرَوْنَها** 'He raised the heavens without any pillars that you could see' (13:2)?" He said: "**سبحان الله أليس الله يقول : بغير عمد ترونها؟** (Glory be to Allah! Does Allah not say: 'without any pillars that you could see'?)" I said: "Yes." He said: "**فثم عمد ولكن لا ترونها** (Then there are pillars, but you do not see them)."

I said: "How is that, may Allah make me your ransom?" He spread his left palm and then placed the right upon it and said: "**هذه أرض الدنيا والسماء الدنيا فوقها قبة ، والأرض الثانية فوق السماء الدنيا والسماء الثانية فوقها قبة ، والأرض الثالثة فوق السماء الثانية والسماء الثالثة فوقها قبة ، والأرض الرابعة فوق السماء الثالثة والسماء الرابعة فوقها قبة ، والأرض الخامسة فوق السماء الرابعة والسماء الخامسة فوقها قبة ، والأرض السادسة فوق السماء الخامسة والسماء السادسة فوقها قبة ، والأرض السابعة فوق السماء السادسة والسماء السابعة فوقها قبة وعرش الرحمن تبارك وتعالى فوق السماء السابعة وهو قول الله عز وجل : الَّذِي خَلَقَ سَبْعَ سَماواتٍ وَمِنَ الْأَرْضِ مِثْلَهُنَّ يَتَنَزَّلُ الْأَمْرُ بَيْنَهُنَ** (This is the earth of the world and the lower heaven is a dome over it; the second earth is above the lower heaven and the second heaven is a dome over it; the third earth is above the second heaven and the third heaven is a dome over it; the fourth earth is above the third heaven and the fourth heaven is a dome over it; the fifth earth is above the fourth heaven and the fifth heaven is a dome over it; the sixth earth is above the fifth heaven and the sixth heaven is a dome over it; the seventh earth is above the sixth heaven and the seventh heaven is a dome over it; and the Throne of the All-Beneficent, Blessed and Exalted is He, is above the seventh heaven. This is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: 'It is Allah who has created seven heavens, and of the earth [a number] similar to them. The command gradually descends through them.')"

"**فأما صاحب الأمر فهو رسول الله صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله ـ والوصي بعد رسول الله قائم على وجه الأرض ـ فإنما يتنزل الأمر إليه من فوق السماء ـ من بين السماوات والأرضين** (As for the Master of the Command (صاحب الأمر), he is the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله)—and the Successor (الوصي) after the Messenger of Allah is established upon the face of the earth—the command only descends to him from above the heaven, from between the heavens and the earths)."

I said: "So there is nothing beneath us but one earth?" He said: "**ما تحتنا إلا أرض واحدة وإن الست لهن فوقنا** (There is nothing beneath us but one earth, and the [other] six are above us)."

I say: Al-Tabarsi narrated from al-Ayyashi from al-Husayn ibn Khalid from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him) the like of it.

The tradition is rare (نادر) in its category. It—and especially what is in its conclusion regarding the descent of the command—is closer to being interpreted according to its inner meaning (المعنى) than being taken literally as a physical form (الصورة). And Allah knows best.
